meta.title: SUDEP-3 Inventory
meta.outcome: A three-item risk-stratification inventory for sudden unexpected death in epilepsy (SUDEP), scoring generalized tonic-clonic seizure frequency, any recent seizures, and intellectual disability, that outperformed the older SUDEP-7 inventory in a case-control validation.
meta.cff_info:
What this shows. This is the SUDEP-3 inventory of Tarighati Rasekhi et al. (Epilepsia 2021), a short three-item scale for stratifying the risk of sudden unexpected death in epilepsy (SUDEP). It was derived from the three components of the older seven-item SUDEP-7 inventory that independently predicted SUDEP, and it outperformed SUDEP-7 (AUC 0.75 vs 0.66).

The three items

  • More than 3 generalized tonic-clonic (GTC) seizures in the last year — 1 point.
  • Any seizure of any type in the last year — 2 points.
  • Intellectual disability (IQ <70 or too impaired to test) — 1 point.

The total score ranges from 0 to 4. Higher scores indicate higher relative SUDEP risk — in the validation cohort each additional point raised the odds of SUDEP by about 180%.

How to interpret

  • A cut-off of ≥2 gave the best diagnostic odds (sensitivity 96%, specificity 34%, diagnostic odds ratio 13.9).
  • A cut-off of ≥3 gave the best overall balance of sensitivity and specificity (Youden's J 0.32; sensitivity 57%, specificity 75%).
  • Two of the three factors — GTC and overall seizure frequency — are modifiable, so the inventory can direct attention to treatable risk.

Important. This is a relative risk-stratification tool from a small case-control study (28 SUDEP cases, 56 matched controls); it does not give an individual's absolute probability of SUDEP (which is on the order of ~1 per 1000 people with epilepsy per year). It awaits validation in larger population cohorts. Demonstration and counseling aid only — not a substitute for specialist assessment.

predictors.name: More than 3 GTC seizures in the last year
predictors.assess: More than three generalized tonic-clonic (GTC) seizures in the past year. GTC seizure frequency is the strongest single SUDEP risk factor in this and most prior studies (>3 GTCs: SUDEP-3 weight 1 point; univariate OR 4.7, 95% CI 1.8–12.7).

predictors.name: Any seizure in the last year
predictors.assess: One or more seizures of any type in the past year (excluding pure absence seizures, which do not confer SUDEP risk). Carries the largest weight in the inventory (2 points; univariate OR 13.9, 95% CI 1.7–110.0).

predictors.name: Intellectual disability
predictors.assess: Intellectual disability, defined as an IQ below 70 or being too impaired to test. A significant, independent SUDEP risk factor (1 point; univariate OR 4.3, 95% CI 1.1–16.4), possibly reflecting a propensity to post-ictal central apnea.

recommendation.text: Lower SUDEP-3 tier (below the optimal ≥2 cut-off). Note the inventory is highly sensitive — a score of ≥1 still captured 96% of SUDEP cases (specificity 32%) — so a low score is reassuring but does not exclude risk.

Meets the optimal SUDEP-3 cut-off (≥2): best diagnostic odds in the validation study (sensitivity 96%, specificity 34%, diagnostic odds ratio 13.9). Elevated relative risk — prioritise treating the modifiable factors (GTC and overall seizure frequency).

Meets the higher-specificity SUDEP-3 cut-off (≥3): optimal balance of sensitivity and specificity (Youden's J 0.32; sensitivity 57%, specificity 75%). High-risk tier — intensify seizure control and counsel on SUDEP risk reduction.

model.note: The SUDEP-3 score (0–4) is a relative risk-stratification inventory, not an absolute probability of SUDEP. Each additional point raised the odds of SUDEP by ~180% in the validation cohort (AUC 0.75).

provenance.source: Tarighati Rasekhi et al., Epilepsia 2021;62(7):1536-1545, Table 3 (SUDEP-3 item weightings) and Figure 3 (ROC / cut-off analysis) — doi 10.1111/epi.16928.
provenance.notes: Three-item additive inventory (max score 4): >3 GTC seizures/yr = 1pt, any seizure/yr = 2pt, intellectual disability = 1pt (Table 3 weightings, from B-coefficients 0.98/2.13/1.15 rounded to nearest integer). Cut-off operating characteristics from Fig-3 SUDEP-3 analysis: ≥2 sens 0.964/spec 0.339/DOR 13.87 (optimal DOR); ≥3 sens 0.571/spec 0.750/Youden 0.321 (optimal J); ≥4 spec 1.00. AUC 0.75 (vs SUDEP-7 0.66); OR per point ~2.8 (odds +180%). Built as a recommendation-score (no absolute-probability output) because the study is 1:2 case-control (28 SUDEP : 56 controls) and reports no calibrated intercept — an absolute SUDEP probability would be misleading (true SUDEP incidence ~1.2/1000/yr). Extracted 2026-07-08.
